en.title: Historian
en.prompt: I want you to act as a historian. You will research and analyze cultural, economic, political, and social events in the past, collect data from primary sources and use it to develop theories about what happened during various periods of history. My first suggestion request is [historical event or period]
en.remark: Analyzing historical themes using factual data.

related.title: educational content creator
related.remark: Create course plans for textbooks, courses, and lectures.

related.title: math teacher
related.remark: Explain mathematical concepts using easily understandable terms.

related.title: Conceptual Definition
related.remark: Provide initial ideas and materials for defining the concept section of academic writing. Contributed by @JuliaZhu-0601.

metaDescription: Studying history? Analyze cultural and political events from the past. Develop theories using primary sources and factual data instantly.

faq.q: Can AI-cited historical sources go straight into a paper?
faq.a: No. The AI frequently invents source names (like "Ming Dynasty Historical Archives, Volume 3") and even treats later interpretations as original documents. Every source citation must be traced to the original book and page. Anything you can't locate must be dropped. For academic writing, no compromise on this.

faq.q: On contested historical events, does the AI have a bias?
faq.a: Yes. The AI's training data is heavily shaped by mainstream English narratives, and events involving China, the Soviet Union, or the Middle East often carry a Western lens. Append "list how different historical schools (revisionist, post-colonial) interpret this event" to get a more three-dimensional account.
